title: SyncLagError.lag is a redundant public field that allows inconsistent construction
description: SyncLagError is a pub struct with pub fields {scan_tip, chain_tip, lag}. lag is always chain_tip.saturating_sub(scan_tip) — it is fully derived from the other two fields. Exposing it as a pub field means external code can construct SyncLagError { scan_tip: 0, chain_tip: 10, lag: 99 } with an inconsistent lag value. Fix: make lag a method — fn lag(&self) -> u64 { self.chain_tip.saturating_sub(self.scan_tip) } — and remove the field. The Display impl and all tests compute lag from the other two fields, so this is a mechanical change.
